questions.question: Describe Mahendra’s job and lifestyle.
questions.answer: Mahendra was a junior supervisor who worked at different construction sites. His job required him to move from place to place such as mines, bridges and factories. Because of this, he often lived in temporary shelters or tents in lonely areas.

questions.question: Explain the character of Iswaran.
questions.answer: Iswaran was Mahendra’s cook and companion. He was hardworking, loyal and imaginative. He loved reading Tamil thrillers and had the ability to tell stories in a dramatic and interesting way.

questions.question: Why was Iswaran considered an asset to Mahendra?
questions.answer: Iswaran helped Mahendra in many ways. He cooked meals, managed household tasks and entertained Mahendra with interesting stories. Because of this, Mahendra never felt lonely.

questions.question: Describe Iswaran’s storytelling style.
questions.answer: Iswaran had a dramatic storytelling style. He described even simple incidents with suspense and excitement. His narration was influenced by the Tamil thriller books he read.

questions.question: Describe how Iswaran narrated the story of the elephant.
questions.answer: Iswaran narrated the story of a mad elephant that escaped from a timber yard and entered a town. The elephant destroyed fences, stalls and objects in a school ground. Iswaran claimed that he stopped the elephant by striking its toenail.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra enjoy listening to Iswaran’s stories?
questions.answer: Mahendra enjoyed listening to Iswaran’s stories because they were interesting and entertaining. Iswaran’s dramatic narration made even ordinary incidents exciting.

questions.question: Explain how Iswaran prepared a special dinner for Mahendra.
questions.answer: One night Iswaran prepared a special dinner because it was an auspicious day. According to tradition, special food was prepared to honour ancestral spirits.

questions.question: What ghost story did Iswaran tell Mahendra?
questions.answer: Iswaran told Mahendra about a frightening female ghost with matted hair holding a foetus. He said that the ghost appeared on full-moon nights near the factory area.

questions.question: How did Mahendra react when he first heard the ghost story?
questions.answer: Mahendra did not believe the story. He laughed and said ghosts were imaginary. He scolded Iswaran for talking nonsense.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra begin to feel uneasy after hearing the ghost story?
questions.answer: Although he did not believe in ghosts, the story affected his mind. The lonely environment and repeated thoughts made him uneasy at night.

questions.question: Describe the incident that happened on the full-moon night.
questions.answer: Mahendra woke up at night after hearing a strange moaning sound. When he looked outside the window, he saw a dark cloudy figure holding something and became frightened.

questions.question: How did Mahendra try to explain the strange sight?
questions.answer: Mahendra tried to calm himself by thinking that it was just imagination or auto-suggestion caused by Iswaran’s stories.

questions.question: What did Iswaran say the next morning?
questions.answer: The next morning Iswaran told Mahendra that he had seen the female ghost during the night, which frightened Mahendra even more.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra decide to leave the place?
questions.answer: Mahendra decided to leave the place because he was deeply frightened by the mysterious incident and Iswaran’s ghost story.

questions.question: Explain the theme of imagination in the story.
questions.answer: The story shows that imagination can influence people’s thoughts and emotions. Iswaran’s stories affected Mahendra’s mind and created fear.

questions.question: How does suspense make the story interesting?
questions.answer: Suspense keeps readers curious and builds tension. The ghost story and mysterious night incident create excitement in the narrative.

questions.question: Describe the setting of the story.
questions.answer: The story takes place in lonely construction sites and temporary shelters. The isolated environment adds mystery and suspense to the story.

questions.question: How does the author show humour in the story?
questions.answer: The author shows humour through Iswaran’s exaggerated stories and Mahendra’s reactions to them.

questions.question: Explain the contrast between Mahendra and Iswaran.
questions.answer: Mahendra is practical and logical, while Iswaran is imaginative and dramatic. Their contrasting personalities make the story interesting.

questions.question: Why is Iswaran considered a fascinating storyteller?
questions.answer: Iswaran is considered fascinating because he narrates stories in a dramatic way and keeps listeners engaged with suspense and imagination.

questions.question: What role does fear play in the story?
questions.answer: Fear plays an important role as it shows how imagination can influence human emotions and behaviour.

questions.question: How does the story highlight the power of storytelling?
questions.answer: The story shows that storytelling can influence people’s thoughts and emotions, as seen in Mahendra’s fear after hearing Iswaran’s ghost story.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach about imagination?
questions.answer: The story teaches that imagination can sometimes create fear and confusion in people’s minds.

questions.question: Why does the story remain interesting to readers?
questions.answer: The story remains interesting because of its suspense, humour, dramatic storytelling and unexpected ending.

questions.question: Summarise the central idea of the story.
questions.answer: The story shows how imagination and storytelling can influence human thoughts and create fear, even in logical people.
